questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A thin uniform square lamina of side $a$ is placed in the $x y$-plane with its sides parallel to $X$ and $Y$-axes and with its centre coinciding with origin. Its moment of inertia about an axis passing through a point on the $Y$-axis at a distance $Y=2 a$ and parallel to $X$-axis is equal to its moment of inertia about an axis passing through a point on the $X$-axis at a distance $x=d$ and perpendicular to $x y$-plane. Then value of $d$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{array}{rlrl}\frac{m a^2}{12}+m\left(4 a^2\right) & =\frac{m a^2}{6}+m d^2 \\ & \text { or } \quad d^2 & =\frac{47 a^2}{12} \\ \therefore & d & =\sqrt{\frac{47}{12}} a\end{array}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A solid cube of wood of side 2a and mass M is resting on a horizontal surface as shown in the figure. The cube is free to rotate about a fixed axis AB. A bullet of mass m(<<M) and speed v is shot horizontally at the face opposite to ABCD at a height of 4a3 from the surface to impart the cube and angular speed ω. It strikes the face and embeds in the cube. Then ω is close to (note: the moment of inertia of the cube about an axis perpendicular to the face and passing through the center of mass is 2Ma23.)
questions.solution:
Applying the parallel axis theorem, I=Icm+Ma2, here Icm is the moment of inertia passing through centre of mass, a is the distance between two axes.

The moment of inertia of solid cube about AB is I=23Ma2+Ma22

I=83Ma2

The cube is free to rotate about AB axis. Thus, the angular momentum should be conserved.

Angular momentum mvr=Iω, here r=4a3.

So, mv×4a3=Iω

⇒ω=mv4a383Ma2=mv2Ma.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
A rectangular plate of length L, width B and mass M rotates with angular velocity ω about an axis in the x-y plane. The axis of rotation passes through the centre of the mass and cuts the length of the plate at an acute angle θ. 

 

Find total angular momentum of the plate about center of mass.
questions.solution:
The angular velocity is given by, 

ω→=ωx i^+ωy j^+ωz k^ 

Where ωx=ωsinθ, ωy=ωcosθ and ωz=0.

Lx=Ixωx=ML212ωsinθ

Ly=Iyωy=MB212ωcosθ

Lz=0

L→=Lxi^+Lyj^+Lzk^

=Mω12L2sinθi^+B2cosθj^
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A Uniform rod AB is hinged at its end A and the other end of the rod is connected to a block through a massless string as shown in the figure. The pulley is smooth and massless. Masses of the block and the rod are same and are equal to m. The acceleration of the block just after the release from this position is
questions.solution:
For the rod: Tℓ-mgℓ2=mℓ23×aℓ ...(i)

For the: mg-T=ma ...(ii)

Solving (i) and (ii) we get: a=3g8
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: From a uniform wire, two circular loops are made (i) $P$ of radius $r$ and (ii) $Q$ of radius $n r$. If the moment of inertia of $Q$ about an axis passing through its centre and perpendicular to its plane is 8 times that of $P$ about a similar axis, the value of $n$ is (diameter of the wire is very much smaller than $i$ or $n r$ )
questions.solution:
Moment of inertia of circular loop,
$I=m r^2$
Given,
$\begin{aligned} I_Q & =8 I_D \\ m r_Q^3 & =8 m_p r_D^2\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} m_p \times\left(n r_p\right)^2 & =8 m_p \times r_p^2 \\ n^2 & =8 \Rightarrow n=2 \sqrt{2}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
A disc of mass $10 \mathrm{~kg}$ and radius $0 \cdot 1 \mathrm{~m}$ is rotating at 120 r.p.m. A retarding torque brings it to rest in $10 \mathrm{~s}$. If the same torque is due to force applied tangentially on
the rim of the disc then magnitude of force is
questions.solution:
frequency $\mathrm{f}=120 \mathrm{rpm}=\frac{120}{60}=2 \mathrm{rps}$
$\omega=2 \pi \mathrm{f}=2 \pi \times 2=4 \pi \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$
Angular acceleration $=\propto=\frac{\omega_{2}-\omega_{1}}{t}=\frac{0-4 \pi}{10}=\frac{-2 \pi}{5} \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$
Moment of inertia $\quad$ I $=\frac{\mathrm{MR}^{2}}{2}=\frac{10 \times(0.1)^{2}}{2}$
$=0.05 \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~m}^{2}$
Torque $\tau=\mathrm{I} \alpha=0.05 \times \frac{2}{5} \pi=0.02 \pi \mathrm{Nm}$.
$\tau=\mathrm{Fr}$
$\therefore \mathrm{F}=\frac{\tau}{\mathrm{r}}=\frac{0.02 \pi}{0.1}=0.2 \pi \mathrm{N}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The ratio of radius of gyration of a ring to that of a disc (both circular) of same radius and mass, about a tangential axis perpendicular to the plane is
questions.solution:
Radius of gyration is given by, $K=\sqrt{\frac{I}{m}}$
We know moment of inertia for the ring $I_{\text {disc }}=\frac{1}{2} m R^2$ and the disc $I_{\text {ring }}=m R^2$ about its central axis perpendicular to its plane.
Using parallel axis theorem, the moment of inertia about a tangential axis perpendicular to the plane can be easily obtained using,
$I^{\prime}=I+m R^2$
The radius of gyration is defined as:
$\begin{aligned}
& K=\sqrt{\frac{I}{m}} \\
& \therefore K_{\text {disc }}=\sqrt{\frac{\frac{1}{2} m R^2+m R^2}{m}}=\sqrt{\frac{3}{2}} R \\
& \therefore K_{\text {ring }}=\sqrt{\frac{m R^2+m R^2}{m}}=\sqrt{2} R
\end{aligned}$
Thus, the ratio of the radius of gyration of a ring to that of a disc of same radius and mass, about a tangential axis perpendicular to the plane is:
$\frac{K_{\text {disc }}}{K_{\text {ring }}}=\frac{\sqrt{3 / 2}}{\sqrt{2}}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A disc of the moment of inertia <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>I</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> is rotating in a horizontal plane about an axis passing through a centre and perpendicular to its plane with constant angular speed <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ω</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> Another disc of the moment of inertia <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>I</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> having zero angular speed is placed coaxially on a rotating disc. Now both the disc are rotating with the constant angular speed <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ω</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>. The energy lost by the initial rotating disc is
questions.solution:
I1ω1=I1+I2ω2
ω2ω1=I1I1+I2
E1-E2
=12I1ω12-12(I1+I2)ω22
=12ω12I1-I1+I2ω22ω12 
=12ω12I1-I1+I2I12I1+I22 
=12ω12I12+I1I2-I12I1+I2=12I1I2I1+I2ω12
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The diameter of a flywheel is $1 \mathrm{~m}$. It has a mass of $20 \mathrm{~kg}$. It is rotating about its axis with a speed of 120 rotations in one minute. Its angular momentum in $\mathrm{kg}-\mathrm{m}^2 / \mathrm{s}$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & r=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~m}=0.5 \mathrm{~m}, m=20 \mathrm{~kg}, \\ & n=120 \text { rotation per minute, } \\ &=2 \text { rotation per sec. } \\ & \text { Angular momentum } L=I \omega \\ &=\frac{M r^2}{2} 2 \pi n=\frac{20 \times(0.5)^2}{2} \times 2 \times 3.14 \times 2 \\ &=5 \times 3.14 \times 2=31.4 \mathrm{~kg}-\mathrm{m}^2 / \mathrm{s}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Four identical uniform solid spheres each of same mass ' $M$ ' and radius ' $R$ ' are placed touching each other as shown in figure with centres A, B, C, D. $\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{A}}, \mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{B}}, \mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{C}}, \mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{D}}$ are the moment of inertia of these spheres respectively about an axis passing through centre and perpendicular to the plane, then
questions.solution:
$\therefore \quad$ The moment of inertia of $\mathrm{A}$ and $\mathrm{D}$ will be equal as the mass, distance, and position are similar. Similarly, the moment of inertia of B and C are equal.
$\therefore \quad$ But the moment of inertia of A and D is greater than $\mathrm{B}$ and $\mathrm{C}$ as they are at the ends.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>V</mi></math> shaped rigid body has two identical uniform arms. What must be the angle between the two arms so that when the body is hung from one end, the other arm is horizontal?
questions.solution:
Let the length of each arm be l.

 For one arm to be horizontal, the total torque acting about O should be zero, thus OP=OQ.

Here the angle is θ between the two arms.

From the figure, we have OQ=l2cosθ.

As seen from the figure, AE=AC+CE

AE=lcosθ+OQ

l2=lcosθ+l2cosθ

cosθ=13⇒θ=cos-113
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A particle starts from rest and performing circular motion of constant radius with speed given by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>v</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>α</mi><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> is a constant and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is the distance covered. The correct graph of magnitude of its tangential acceleration <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><msub><mi>a</mi><mi>t</mi></msub></mfenced></math> and centripetal acceleration <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><msub><mi>a</mi><mi>c</mi></msub></mfenced></math> versus <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi></math> will be
questions.solution:
In circular motion, tangential acceleration is obtained due to change in speed while radial acceleration is obtained due to change in direction, i.e.,
at =dvdt = dvdx.dxdt =vdvdx
and, ar = ω2r = v2r

as, v=αx⇒dxdt=αx⇒x=α2t24

at=vdvdx=αx·α2x=α22=constant.
So, the graph will be a straight line, parallel to time axis.

Now,
ac=v2r=α2xr=α2r×α2t24⇒ac∝t2So, the graph will be parabolic, opening upwards.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A circular platform is free to rotate in a horizontal plane about a vertical axis passing through its centre. A tortoise is sitting at the edge of the platform. Now the platform is given an angular velocity <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">ω</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>0</mn></mrow></msub></math>. When the tortoise moves along a chord of the platform with a constant velocity (w.r.t. the platform), which of the following graphs shows properly the variation of angular velocity of the platform with the time <math><mi>t</mi></math>?
questions.solution:
As there is no external torque, angular momentum will remain constant. When the tortoise moves from A to C, figure, moment of inertia of the platform and tortoise decreases. Therefore, angular velocity of the system increases. When the tortoise moves from C to B, moment of inertia increases. Therefore, angular velocity decreases



If, M=mass of platform

R=radius of platform

m=mass of tortoise moving along the chord AB

a=perpendicular distance of O from AB

Initial angular momentum, I1=mR2+MR22

At any time t, let the tortoise reach D moving with velocity v

∴ AD=vt

AC=R2-a2

As DC=AC-AD=(R2-a2-vt)

∴  OD=r=a2+R2-a2-vt2

Angular momentum at time t

I2=mr2+MR22

As angular momentum is conserved

∴  I1ω0=I2ωt

This shows that variation of ω(t) with time is nonlinear. Choice
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Three identical uniform thin metal rods form the three sides of an equilateral triangle. If the moment of inertia of the system of these three rods about an axis passing through the centroid of the triangle and perpendicular to the plane of the triangle is $n$ times. The moment of inertia of one rod separately about an axis passing through the centre of the rod and perpendicular to its length, the value of $n$ is
questions.solution:
Let the length of the rod be $l$.
The moment of inertia of one rod separately about an axis passing through the centre of the rod and perpendicular to its length, $\mathrm{I}=\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{Ac}}+m r^2$ $\begin{aligned} & =\frac{1}{12} m l^2+m\left(\frac{l}{2 \sqrt{3}}\right)^2 \\ & =\frac{m l^2}{12}+\frac{m l^2}{12}=\frac{2 m l^2}{12} \end{aligned}$ Moment of inertia of the system of 3rods $=3 \times \frac{2 m l^2}{12}=6 \times \frac{m l^2}{12}=6 l$ According to the question $\frac{6 m l^2}{12}=l=n \cdot\left(\frac{m l^2}{12}\right)$ $n=6$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Moment of inertia <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>I</mi></math> of a solid sphere about an axis parallel to diameter and at distance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> from its centre of mass varies as
questions.solution:
Ixx = I = Ic + mx2
             (m = mas of sphere)
               I =Ic
at            x = 0
Therefore, I versus x graph is a
parabola with minimum value of
I = Ic at x = 0. Therefore, value of moment of inertia will increase from a minimum value as a parabolic curve.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A stationary body explodes into four identical fragments such that three of them fly off mutually perpendicular to each other, each with a kinetic energy <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msub><mi>E</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> . The total energy of explosion is
questions.solution:
It is given that a stationary body explodes into four identical fragments such that three of them fly oft mutually perpendicular to each other, each with kinetic energy E02 .

Let the speed of each of them be v

So. 12 mv2=E02

Using the principle of conservation of energy, the speed of the fourth fragment

v'=v2+v2+v2=3 v

Its kinetic energy,

E'=12 m 3 v2=32 mv2=32 E0

Hence the total energy produced by the explosion is,

E'+3×E02=32E0+32E0=3 E0
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Moment of inertia of a body about an axis is $4 \mathrm{~kg}-\mathrm{m}^2$. The body is initially at rest and a torque of $8 \mathrm{~N}$-m starts acting on it along the same axis. Work done by the torque in $20 \mathrm{~s}$, in joules, is
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
From a uniform circular thin disc of mass $9 \mathrm{M}$ and radius $\mathrm{R}$, a small disc of radius $\frac{\mathrm{R}}{3}$
is removed. The centre of the small disc is at a distance $\frac{2 \mathrm{R}}{3}$ from the centre of
original disc. The moment of inertia of the remaining disc about an axis
perpendicular to the plane of the disc and passing through the centre of the disc
of radius $\mathrm{R}$ is
questions.solution: The moment of inertia of the complete disc $\mathrm{I}_{1}=\frac{9 \mathrm{MR}^{2}}{2}$ The moment of inertia of the removed disc $=$ $$ \begin{aligned} I^{\prime} &=\frac{M}{2}\left(\frac{R}{3}\right)^{2}+M\left(\frac{2 R}{3}\right)^{2}=\frac{M R^{2}}{18}+\frac{4 M R^{2}}{9} \\ &=\frac{M R^{2}}{2} \end{aligned} $$ [Since mass is proportional to area, it is proportional to square of the radius. Since the radius of the removed disc is $\frac{R}{3}$, its mass will be $M$ ] $\therefore$ Moment of inertia of the remaining disc will be $I_{2}=I_{1}-I^{\prime}=\frac{9 m R^{2}}{2}-\frac{M R^{2}}{2}=4 M R^{2}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A uniform rod of length ‘ 6L ’ and mass ‘ 8m ’ is pivoted at its centre ‘ C ’. Two masses ‘ m ’ and ‘ 2m ’ with speed 2v, and v, as shown strikes the rod and stick to the rod. Initially, the rod is at rest. Due to impact, if it rotates with angular velocity ‘ ω1 ’, then ‘ ω ’ will be.
questions.solution:
From angular momentum conservation:
2mvL+m2v2L=8m6L122+2mL2+m2L2ω
⇒ω=v5L
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Three points masses, each of mass 'm' are placed at the corners of an equilateral
triangle of side $\ell^{\prime}$. The moment of inertia of the system about an axis passing
through one of the vertices and parallel to the side joining other two vertices, will be
questions.solution: (D) $\begin{aligned} I &=2 \mathrm{mh}^{2} \\ & \mathrm{~h}=\ell \sin 60^{\circ}=\ell \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \\ I &=2 \mathrm{~m} \times \frac{3}{4} \cdot \ell^{2}=\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{~m} \ell^{2} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A disc of radius 0.4 meter and mass $1 \mathrm{~kg}$ rotates about an axis passing through its center and perpendicular to its plane. The angular acceleration is $10 \mathrm{rad} \mathrm{s}^{-2}$. The tangential force applied to the rim of the disc is
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{R}=0.4 \mathrm{~m}, \mathrm{M}=1 \mathrm{~kg}, \alpha=10 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}^2$
$\mathrm{I}=\frac{\mathrm{MR}^2}{2}=\frac{1 \times(0.4)^2}{2}=0.08 \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~m}^2$
Torque, $\tau=\mathrm{I} \alpha=0.08 \times 10=0.8 \mathrm{~N}-\mathrm{m}$
Also, $\tau=$ FR
or $F=\frac{\tau}{R}=\frac{0.8}{0.4}=2 \mathrm{~N}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Three solid spheres each of mass m and diameter d are stuck together such that the lines connecting the centres form an equilateral triangle of side length d. Find the ratio I0/IA, where I0 is the moment of inertia of the system about an axis passing through the centroid and IA is the moment of inertia of the system about an axis passing through the centre of any sphere and perpendicular to the plane of the triangle.
questions.solution:
The moment of inertia of the system about the point O is

I0=325Md22+Md32=1310Md2

The moment of inertia of the system about the point A is

IA=225Md22+M(d)2+25Md22=2310Md2

So, the ratio =1323
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A solid ball rolls down a parabolic path ABC from a height h as shown in the figure. The portion AB of the path is rough while BC is smooth. How high will the ball climb in BC?
questions.solution:
Using the conservation of mechanical energy between A and B

At B, total kinetic energy = mgh

Here,  m = mass of the ball

The ratio of rotational to translational kinetic energy would be,

         K⁡ R⁡ K⁡ T⁡ = 2 5

∴     K⁡R⁡=27mgh⁡andK⁡T⁡=57mgh

In portion BC, friction is absent. Therefore, rotational kinetic energy will remain constant and translational kinetic energy will convert into potential energy.

Hence, if H be the height to which ball climbs in BC, then

       mgH = KT

or    mgH=57mgh⁡orH⁡=57h⁡
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A uniform thin rod of $120 \mathrm{~cm}$ length and $1600 \mathrm{~g}$ mass is bent as shown in the figure. The moment of inertia of the bent rod about an axis passing through the point ' $O$ ' and perpendicular to the plane of the paper is $\mathrm{kg}-\mathrm{m}^2$.
questions.solution:
There are four rods each of mass $\mathrm{m}=400 \mathrm{~g}=0.4 \mathrm{~kg}$ and each having a length of $\ell=30 \mathrm{~cm}=0.3 \mathrm{~m}$ So, we can calculate moment of inertia as follows
$\begin{aligned} & h^2=l^2-\left(\frac{l}{2}\right)^2=\frac{3}{4} l^2 \\ & \text { M.I. system }=(\text { MI of I }) \times 2+(\text { MI of II }) \times 2 \\ & =\frac{m l^2}{3} \times 2+2\left(\frac{m l^2}{12}+m h^2\right) \\ & =\frac{2}{3} m l^2+2\left(\frac{m l^2}{12}+\frac{3}{4} m l^2\right) \\ & =\frac{7}{3} m l^2=\frac{7}{3} \times 0.4 \times 0.3 \times 0.3=0.084 \mathrm{~kg}-\mathrm{m}^2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A pendulum is oscillating with an angular amplitude of 90° as shown in the figure. The value of angle α, made by the string with the vertical, at which the acceleration is directed horizontally is
questions.solution:
For horizontal acceleration, net force in vertical direction should be zero.



Loss in potential energy = gain in kinetic energy

mgh=12mv2

mglcos⁡α=12mv2

∴ v=2glcos⁡α

∵ The net force towards O is

T-mgcos⁡α=mv2l

=m2glcos⁡α l=2 mgcos⁡α

∴T=3mgcos⁡α

∴Tcos⁡α=mg

Or 3mg cos2 α=mg

∴α=cos-113
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Consider a sphere of mass $M$ and radius $R$ centered at origin. The density of material of the sphere is $\rho=A r^\alpha$, where $r$ is the radial distance, $\alpha$ and $A$ are constants. If the moment of inertia of the sphere about the axis passing through centre is $\frac{6}{7} M R^2$, then the value of $\alpha$ is
questions.solution:
Given, density of sphere, $p=A r^\alpha$ (where, $r=$ radial distance and $A$ and $\alpha$ are constants) Consider an elemental spherical shell of radius $r$ and thickness $d r$
Mass of elemental spherical shell, $$ \begin{aligned} & d m=\text { Volume } \times \text { Density } \\ & d m=\left(4 \pi r^2\right) d r \cdot A r^\alpha=4 \pi A r^{2+\alpha} d r \end{aligned} $$ Mass of entire solid sphere, $$ \begin{aligned} & M=4 \pi A \int_0^R r^{2+\alpha} d r \\ & M=4 \pi A\left[\frac{r^{3+\alpha}}{3+\alpha}\right]_0^R=\frac{4 \pi A}{3+\alpha} \cdot R^{3+\alpha} \end{aligned} $$ Now, moment of inertia of elemental spherical shell is $$ d I=\frac{2}{3}(d m) \cdot r^2=\frac{2}{3}\left(4 \pi A r^{2+\alpha} d r\right) r^2 $$ Moment of inertia of entire solid sphere, $$ I=\int_0^R d I=\frac{2}{3} 4 \pi A \int_0^R r^{4+\alpha} \cdot d r $$ $$ \begin{aligned} & I=\frac{2}{3} 4 \pi A\left[\frac{r^{5+\alpha}}{5+\alpha}\right]_0^R \Rightarrow I=\frac{2}{3} 4 \pi A\left[\frac{R^{5+\alpha}}{5+\alpha}\right] \\ & I=\frac{2}{3}\left(\frac{4 \pi A}{3+\alpha} \cdot R^{3+\alpha}\right) \cdot \frac{R^2(3+\alpha)}{5+\alpha} \end{aligned} $$ From Eq. (ii), we get $$ I=\frac{2}{3} M R^2 \cdot\left(\frac{3+\alpha}{5+\alpha}\right) $$ It is given in the question, $$ I=\frac{6}{7} M R^2 $$ On comparing Eqs (iii) and (iv), we get $$ \begin{aligned} \frac{2}{3} M R^2 \cdot\left(\frac{3+\alpha}{5+\alpha}\right) & =\frac{6}{7} M R^2 \Rightarrow \frac{3+\alpha}{5+\alpha}=\frac{9}{7} \\ 21+7 \alpha & =45+9 \alpha \\ \alpha \quad \alpha & =-12 \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A semicircular plate of mass \(m\) has radius \(r\) and centre \(c\). The centre of mass of the plate is at a distance \(x\) from its centre \(c\). Its moment of inertia about an axis passing through its centre of mass and perpendicular to its plane is
questions.solution:
Given, mass of a semicircular plate \(=m\) radius of semicircular plate \(=r\) According to the question we can drawn the following diagram,
Now, from the parallel axis theorem, \(\begin{aligned} I_c & =I_{\mathrm{cm}}+m x^2 \\ I_{\mathrm{cm}} & =I_c-m x^2 \\ \text{Here, } I_c & =\frac{m r^2}{2} \end{aligned}\) Hence, moment of inertia of semicircular plate about an axis passing through its centre of mass and perpendicular to it's plane is \(I_{\mathrm{cm}}=\frac{m r^2}{2}-m x^2\)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: When a 12000 joule of work is done on a flywheel, its frequency of rotation increases from 10 Hz to 20 Hz. The moment of inertia of flywheel about its axis of rotation is <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>π</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>
questions.solution:
Given, work done, W = 12000 J,
Initial frequency, f1=10Hz
Angular velocity for rotational motion is given by
ω=2πf
∴ω1=2πf1=2π×10=20πrads
andω2=2πf2=2π×20=40πrads
According to work-energy theorem,
Work done in rotation = change in rotational kinetic energy
⇒W=12ω22-12ω12∵KErotational12ω2
= 12/ω22-ω12 …(i)
Where, l = moment of inertia of the flywheel.
Substituting given values in Eq. (i), we get
12000121600π2-400π2
⇒=111200×10∵π2=10
⇒l=12000×212000=2kgm2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In the diagram shown, a rod of mass M has been fixed on a ring of the same mass. The whole system has been placed on a perfectly rough surface. The system is gently displaced so that the ring starts rolling. The velocity of the centre of the ring when the rod becomes horizontal is (the length of the rod is equal to the radius of the ring)
questions.solution:
The moment of inertia of the system about instantaneous axis of rotation is given by,

I=Iring+Irod

⇒I=(MR2+MR2)+112MR2+MR12       (From parallel axis theorem)

⇒I=2MR2+112MR2+M5R22           ∵R1=R2+R22=5R2

⇒I=2MR2+112MR2+54MR2=2MR2+43MR2=103MR2

Hence, Isystem=103MR2.


Now from work - energy theorem, we get,

MgR2=Rotational kinetic energy of the system about instantaneous axis of rotation
(here R2 is the distance through which the center of mass of the rod descends)

⇒MgR2=12 Isystem ω2

⇒MgR2=12×103MR2×ω2

⇒ω=3g10R


Now velocity of center of mass is given as, v=Rω.
⇒v=R×3g10R
⇒v=3gR10
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q:
A small ball B of mass m is suspended with light inelastic string of length L from a block A of same mass m which can move on smooth horizontal surface as shown in the figure. The ball is displaced by angle θ  from equilibrium position & then released.



The displacement of centre of mass of A + B system till the string becomes vertical is
questions.solution:
ΔyCM=-mL1-cos θ+0m+m

= L 2 1 - cos  θ
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A solid cylinder of mass \(M\) and radius \(R\) rolls down an inclined plane of length \(L\) and height \(h\), without slipping. Find the speed of its centre of mass when the cylinder reaches its bottom.
questions.solution:
The given situation is shown in the following figure.
When cylinder reaches at bottom, then its whole potential energy is converted into its rotational kinetic energy and linear kinetic energy of its centre of mass. \(\begin{aligned} \text {Hence, } & M g h=\frac{1}{2} I \omega^2+\frac{1}{2} M v_{\mathrm{COM}}^2 \\ &=\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{M R^2}{2} \times\left(\frac{v_{\mathrm{COM}}}{R}\right)^2+\frac{1}{2} M v_{\mathrm{COM}}^2 \\ & \quad\left[\because I=\frac{M R^2}{2} \text { and } \omega=\frac{v_{\mathrm{COM}}}{R}\right] \\ &=\frac{M v_{\mathrm{COM}}^2}{4}+\frac{M v_{\mathrm{COM}}^2}{2} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad M g h=\frac{3}{4} M v_{\mathrm{COM}}^2 \end{aligned}\) \(\begin{array}{ll} \Rightarrow & v_{\text {COM }}^2=\frac{4 g h}{3} \\ \Rightarrow & v_{\text {COM }}=\sqrt{\frac{4 g h}{3}} \end{array}\)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A spool of mass M and radius 2R lies on an inclined plane as shown in figure. A light thread is wound around the connecting tube of the spool and its free end carries a weight of mass m. The value of m so that system is in equilibrium is
questions.solution:
Equilibrium of m give

T=mg         (T = Tension in string)

Net torque about point of contact of the spool should be
zero. Hence,

(2R)(Mgsinα) = TR

or  2Mgsinα = mg

or  m = 2Msinα
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A simple pendulum of length $2 \mathrm{~m}$ is given a horizontal push through angular displacement of $60^{\circ}$. If the mass of bob is 200 gram, the angular velocity of the bob will be (Take Acceleration due to gravity $=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ ) $\left(\sin 30^{\circ}=\cos 60^{\circ}=0.5, \cos 30^{\circ}=\sin 60^{\circ}=\sqrt{3} / 2\right)$
questions.solution:
Given: $l=2 \mathrm{~m}, \theta=60^{\circ}, \mathrm{m}=200 \mathrm{~g}=2 \mathrm{~g}$
From the figure, $\mathrm{T}=\mathrm{mr} \omega^2$... (i) also $\mathrm{T} \cos \theta=\mathrm{mg}$... (ii) putting (i) into (ii) $\mathrm{mr} \omega^2-\cos \theta=\mathrm{mg}$... (iii) putting the given values into equation (iii) $\begin{aligned} & 2 \times 2 \times \omega^2 \frac{1}{2}=2 \times 10 \quad \ldots .\left(\because \cos 60=\frac{1}{2}\right) \\ & \omega^2=10 \\ & \Rightarrow \omega=\sqrt{10} \quad \ldots .(\because \sqrt{10}=\sqrt{2 \times 2 \times 2.5}) \\ & \quad=2 \sqrt{2.5} \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The potential energy of a particle in a central field has the form <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>U</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>r</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msup><mi>r</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>r</mi></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><mi>r</mi><mo>'</mo></math> is the distance from the centre of the field. The magnitude of the maximum attractive force in Newton is
questions.solution:
The relation between central force and potential energy is, 
F=-dUdr, given value of potential energy of a particle in a central field, 
Ur=1r2-1r, it means, 
F=-d1r2-1rdr=-2r3+1r2, 

for maximum force, d-2r3+1r2dr=0⇒3r4-2r3=0⇒r=32, 

⇒F=127
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A mass $M$ is supported by a massless string wound around a uniform cylinder of mass $M$ and radius $R$. On releasing the mass from rest, it will fall with acceleration
questions.solution:
The given situation is shown in the figure,
We know that, torque, $\tau=I \alpha$ If $T$ is the tension in the string, then $$ \tau=T R $$ From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get $$ I \alpha=T R $$ Under no slipping condition, $$ \alpha=\frac{a}{R} $$ Hence, $\quad I \cdot \frac{a}{R}=T R \Rightarrow T=\frac{I \cdot a}{R^2}$ For cylinder, $I=\frac{M R^2}{2}$ $$ \therefore \quad T=\frac{M R^2}{2} \cdot \frac{a}{R^2} \Rightarrow T=\frac{m a}{2} $$ For the motion of the block, $$ \begin{aligned} & M g-T=M a \\ & \Rightarrow \quad M g-\frac{M a}{2}=M a \\ & \Rightarrow \quad M g=\frac{3 M a}{2} \Rightarrow a=\frac{2 g}{3} \\ & \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A spool of mass M=3 kg and radius R=20 cm has an axle of radius r=10 cm around which a string is wrapped. The moment of inertia about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the spool and passing through the centre is MR22. If the coefficient of friction between the surface and the spool is 0.4 then the maximum tension which can be applied to the string for which the spool doesn't slip, is g=10 m s-2
questions.solution:
Writing the torque about the instantaneous axis of rotation we get,

τ=T×R-r=MR22+MR2α

⇒T=3MR22R-raR

Also,

T-μMg=Ma

Substituting the values and solving for T we get,

T=18 N
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
A constant torque of $200 \mathrm{~N}$ turns a flywheel, which is at rest, about an axis through
its centre and perpendicular to its plane. If its moment of inertia is $50 \mathrm{~kg}-\mathrm{m}^{2}$, then
in 4 second, what will be change in its angular momentum?
questions.solution:
(B)
Change in angular momentum $=\tau . t$
$=200 \times 4=800 \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~m}^{2} / \mathrm{s}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: One fourth length of a uniform rod is placed on a rough horizontal surface and rod starts rotating about the edge. At an angle θ between the rod and the horizontal, rod begins to slide on the edge. If coefficient of friction between rod and surface is μ , and x tan θ = 4 μ . Find x. (Take g = 10 m/s2)
questions.solution:
One fourth length

mg ℓ 4 sin θ = 1 2 I ω 2

α = mg ℓ 4 cos θ I

    

μ N - mg sin θ = m ω 2 ℓ 4

mg cos θ - N = m ℓ 4 α

Solving , 1 3 tan θ = 4 μ
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A wheel of mass $20 \mathrm{~kg}$ and radius $30 \mathrm{~cm}$ is rotating at an angular speed of $80 \mathrm{rev} / \mathrm{min}$ when the motor is turned off. Neglecting the friction at the axis, calculate the force that must be applied tangentially to the wheel to bring it to rest in 5 rev.
questions.solution:
Given that, mass of wheel, $m=20 \mathrm{~kg}$
Radius, $R=30 \mathrm{~cm}=30 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~m}$
Initial angular speed, $\begin{aligned} \omega_0 & =80 \mathrm{rpm}=\frac{80 \times 2 \pi}{60} \\ & =\frac{8 \pi}{3} \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}\end{aligned}$
Angular displacement, $\theta=2 \pi \times$ (number of revolution) $=2 \pi \times 5=10 \pi \mathrm{rad}$ Final angular speed, $\omega=0$ By equations of rotational kinematics. Angular acceleration, $\alpha=\frac{\omega^2-\omega_0^2}{2 \theta}$ Substituting the given values, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
\alpha & =\frac{0-\left(\frac{8 \pi}{3}\right)^2}{2 \times 10 \pi} \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}^2 \\
& =-\frac{16 \pi}{45} \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}^2
\end{aligned}
$$
Now, the tangential force will act opposite to the direction of rotation of wheel which will provide necessary retarding torque.
$$
\begin{aligned}
\therefore & & \tau=I \alpha=F R \\
& \Rightarrow & F=\frac{I \alpha}{R}=\frac{\frac{1}{2} m R^2 \alpha}{R}=\frac{1}{2} m R \alpha
\end{aligned}
$$
Substituting the values, we get
$$
F=\frac{1}{2} \times 20 \times 30 \times 10^{-2} \times \frac{16 \pi}{45} \mathrm{~N}=1.06 \pi \mathrm{N}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Two heavy metallic plates are joined together at 90° to each other. A laminar sheet of mass 30 kg is hinged at the line AB joining the two heavy metallic plates. The hinges are frictionless. The moment of inertia of the laminar sheet about an axis parallel to AB and passing through its centre of mass is 1.2 kg - m2. Two rubber obstacles P and Q are fixed, one on each metallic plate at a distance 0.5 m from the line AB. This distance is chosen, so that the reaction due to the hinges on the laminar sheet is zero during the impact. Initially the laminar sheet hits one of the obstacles with an angular velocity 1 rad/s and turns back. If the impulse on the sheet due to each obstacle is 6N-s,


find the location of the centre of mass of the laminar sheet from AB.
questions.solution:
Let the perpendicular distance of CM from the line AB be r and ω the angular velocity of the sheet just after colliding with rubber obstacle for the first time.

Obviously the linear velocity of CM before and after collision will be ν i = r 1 rad / s = r and ν f = r ω .

v i → and v f → will be in opposite directions.

Now, linear impulse on CM = change in linear momentum of CM

or    6 = m ν f + ν i = 30 r + r ω

or      r 1 + ω = 1 5       ....(i)

Similarly, angular impulse about AB = change in angular momentum about AB

Angular impulse = Linear impulse x perpendicular distance of impulse from AB

Hence, 6 0.5 m = I AB ω + 1

(Initial angular velocity = 1 rad/s)

or 3 = I CM + Mr 2 1 + ω

or 3 = 1.2 + 30 r 2 1 + ω      ...(ii)

Solving Eqs. (i) and (ii) for r, we get

r = 0.4 m and r = 0.1 m

But at r = 0.4 m,  ω comes out to be negative (- 0.5 rad/s) which is not acceptable. Therefore,

r = distance of CM from AB = 0.1 m
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
